body:
Just weeks after Rep. Monica De La Cruz vowed to protect in vitro fertilization (IVF), she introduced a budget that would ban IVF for millions of Americans looking to grow their families.

Just weeks after Rep. Monica De La Cruz vowed to protect in vitro fertilization (IVF), she introduced a budget that would ban IVF for millions of Americans looking to grow their families.
**Time and time again, Monica De La Cruz has made it clear she wants to ban all reproductive healthcare. **
Michelle knows that IVF is a treatment that has helped so many families fulfill their dreams of starting a family. All Texans should have the right to choose when and how to start their families.

Monica De La Cruz may try to mislead voters, but the impact of her budget proposals would be devastating to many South Texas families. She has no business interfering in women’s decisions about when and how to grow a family.
The issues on the docket this year have huge consequences — it’s no normal election.
The right for a woman to choose what happens to her own body could be in even more danger if we can’t take back the House. We need a champion who will fearlessly fight to restore abortion care and protect IVF in Texas, and across the country — Michelle Vallejo is that champion.
**[We are counting on you! Please make a contribution to help us protect all ranges of reproductive care, from IVF to abortion.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/81529948/462729070/-141911278?refcode=em-20240323&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85NDUzMSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4MGRiZGRiYi0zZmU5LWVlMTEtYWFmMC0wMDIyNDgyMjM3OTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=HdXkCJ_ABDtuTHpL4FnhRPX75CrsrWPSffL5F9fBBDc=)**

clean_body: Just weeks after Rep. Monica De La Cruz vowed to protect in vitro fertilization (IVF), she introduced a budget that would ban IVF for millions of Americans looking to grow their families. Peter, we can't say we are surprised. Just weeks after Rep. Monica De La Cruz vowed to protect in vitro fertilization (IVF), she introduced a budget that would ban IVF for millions of Americans looking to grow their families. Time and time again, Monica De La Cruz has made it clear she wants to ban all reproductive healthcare. Michelle knows that IVF is a treatment that has helped so many families fulfill their dreams of starting a family. All Texans should have the right to choose when and how to start their families. = If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll instantly process your contribution to Michelle Vallejo: = = = = = = Monica De La Cruz may try to mislead voters, but the impact of her budget proposals would be devastating to many South Texas families. She has no business interfering in women’s decisions about when and how to grow a family. The issues on the docket this year have huge consequences — it’s no normal election. The right for a woman to choose what happens to her own body could be in even more danger if we can’t take back the House. We need a champion who will fearlessly fight to restore abortion care and protect IVF in Texas, and across the country — Michelle Vallejo is that champion. = ¡Vamos por Quince! body:
[ stdin# ][IMG]

INSIDER NEWS FROM THE FRONTLINES OF THE POLITICAL REVOLUTION

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

HEADLINES: Reject AIPAC Coalition Battling $100M Aimed at Progressives |
Half a Million Uncommitted Votes for Peace! | New Endorsement: Susheela
Jayapal for Congress | CA & OH Victories! | & MORE!

★ ★ ★

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/16333?t=1011&akid=5610%2E5885482%2EISWNj7 ]Our Revolution is holding a Member Drive right now to make sure we have
the resources to defend pro-Peace progressives being targeted by Big Money
in 2024. Become a member today with a monthly donation!

★ ★ ★

AIPAC plans to spend $100 MILLION to try to unseat pro-Peace progressives
this election year — and it's already getting started with Congresswoman
Summer Lee’s primary for re-election less than one month out.

Our Revolution is partnering with more than a dozen organizations
nationwide as part of a new Reject AIPAC coalition to fight back. Voters
must know AIPAC is as toxic as the NRA.

AIPAC and its GOP megadonors have no place spending millions in Democratic
primaries & lobbying against Palestinian lives. Join our movement!

Our Revolution Chair Larry Cohen has signed on with Jewish Americans
Opposing AIPAC with a mission to “support candidates who are opposed by
AIPAC and who are advocates for peace and a new, just US policy toward
Israel/Palestine,” [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/16334?t=1012&akid=5610%2E5885482%2EISWNj7 ]The Nation reports.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/16335?t=1013&akid=5610%2E5885482%2EISWNj7 ]Support our work with a $10 one-time gift or $5 monthly donation and
get your “Reject AIPAC” sticker to spread solidarity in the fight against
this toxic right-wing lobbying group!

★ ★ ★

Our Revolution and the Uncommitted coalition have mobilized more than HALF
A MILLION votes for Peace in Michigan, Minnesota, Washington and other
Democratic primaries to urge Biden to push for a permanent ceasefire and
end U.S. funding of Netanyahu’s war in Gaza.

Last week, Washington State added 89,997 uncommitted votes to that total.
Our Revolution was proud to reach out to our 131k members in the state
through text, calls and emails, and we partnered with Rami Alkabra, Deputy
Mayor of Bothell, WA and a Palestinian-American, to get the word out.

In his State of the Union speech, Biden finally called for “an immediate
ceasefire to last 6 weeks” and he has recently criticized Netanyahu,
saying he’s “hurting Israel more than helping.” This is a start, but the
Netanyahu government continues to bomb civilians and block critical
supplies — and, Biden’s floating aid pier off the coast of Gaza will take
30-90 days to construct.

“It’s on the president to make those changes and on the campaign to
adjust, to bring these folks back in so we have the coalition that we need
to be able to beat Trump in November,” Our Revolution Campaigns Director
Paco Fabian told [ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/16361?t=1014&akid=5610%2E5885482%2EISWNj7 ]The Hill.

Let’s maintain our momentum for a PERMANENT ceasefire! Uncommitted votes
in Wisconsin on April 2nd will reinforce the message: It's time for
President Biden to realign with the values of the base.

★ ★ ★

Last week, Our Revolution joined Sen. Bernie Sanders, US Rep. Ro Khanna,
and service workers from around the country for a special Covid Memorial
event hosted by One Fair Wage.

“This memorial service asks us to remember not just the 12,000 service
workers who lost their lives in the pandemic, but all the lives harmed by
the restaurant industry’s exploitative business model built on subminimum
wages, not paying benefits, and leaving workers vulnerable to abuses,”
said Our Revolution Executive Director Joseph Geevarghese.

Bernie said: “What Covid taught us is that if you have a lot of money in
the midst of a terrible pandemic, you can retreat to your home and not get
exposed, but if you’re a working person - in the service industry - there
was no option. You had to go to work and put your life on the line to feed
your family.”

Our Revolution is proud to stand in solidarity to fight state by state to
raise wages and radically reform how the industry treats workers. “‘Our
role is to pray for the dead and fight like hell for the living.’ That’s
what we’re called to do,” Joseph quoted legendary labor leader Mother
Jones.

★ ★ ★

★ ★ ★

NEW ENDORSEMENT!

Our Revolution is proud to endorse Susheela Jayapal for Congress in
Oregon’s 3rd Congressional District, and Bernie Sanders has joined us in
supporting her in this race!

Susheela has been an outspoken champion for affordable housing, education,
economic justice, and a living wage. As a Multnomah County Commissioner,
she fiercely advocated for survivors of domestic violence and solutions to
the homelessness crisis.

In Congress, Susheela will be a strong progressive voice, championing
abortion rights, taking on big polluters and fighting climate change, and
standing up to election deniers to defend our democracy.

★ ★ ★

OHIO VICTORIES

In Ohio, Our Revolution-endorsed Tristan Rader is heading to the general
election in November in his run for State House in District 13.

Tristan has served in dual roles as the Ohio Program Director at Solar
United Neighbors (SUN) and as a deeply engaged member of the Lakewood City
Council.

He is a trailblazer for sustainability and social equity. “We must
champion universal healthcare, uphold workers' rights, defend our
environment, and tirelessly work towards social and economic justice for
every Ohioan,” Tristan said.

“This is not just a political battle; it's a moral imperative,” he said.
“We cannot stand idly by as the far-right’s agenda of bigotry and hatred
defines what it is to be an Ohioan. The time for decisive action is now.”

★ ★ ★

CALIFORNIA VICTORIES

We are excited to announce that Jovanka Beckles won her primary and will
be on the ballot for CA State Senate this November!

Jovanka had our support - and Bernie’s! - in her successful campaigns for
Richmond City Council, including in 2014 when she took on Chevron for
polluting her community and WON!

As a city councilor, she raised the minimum wage. As a Teamsters Local 856
member, she’s on the picket lines at a moment’s notice. As District 7's
next state senator, she will be the courageous leader we need to pass
policies that put people over profit.

“I’m running because we need bold leadership who will be courageous on
climate, healthcare, criminal justice reform, housing, taxing the rich,
women’s and worker’s rights, and more,” Jovanka said.

Our Revolution-endorsed Tina McKinnor will be joining the CA Assembly!
Tina knows how to legislate and get things done for working people.

As Chief of Staff to several Assembly members, she helped pass over 100
bills, including historic legislation that gave the California legislative
staff the right to unionize and collectively bargain for the first time in
the state's history.

Nithya Raman beat out the competition and will be an amazing asset to the
progressive movement as a member of Los Angeles City Council!

Nithya has worked to remedy extreme poverty in India and started a
grassroots volunteer program in her community in Los Angeles, which
brought down the number of unsheltered homeless individuals in her
district for the first time in years.

Mai Vang will be a powerful member of the Sacramento City Council and a
voice for working people, the environment, housing, and peace. This is how
we build local power to directly improve lives in the city of Sacramento.

Right wing forces tried to push tough-on-crime judges, Our Revolution was
pleased to endorse Judges Michael Begert and Patrick Thompson who won
their seats on the San Francisco Superior Court.

★ ★ ★

PARTY SEAT VICTORIES

Our Revolution is working to transform the Democratic Party from the
inside out because we know how important it is to elect progressives to
crucial positions to grow our influence at all levels inside the party.

Three of our candidates won seats on the Democratic Party of San Francisco
County Central Committee - Jon Avalos, Jane Kim, and Connie Chan. These
seats are key to growing progressive influence on the party’s policy
platforms and endorsements.

In Alameda County, CA, 8 of our candidates will be joining the ranks of
the Democratic Party of Alameda County Central Committee.

Each of these progressive committee members brings their life experience
to the table to stand up for our progressive principles: from healthcare
justice to housing and homelessness, we need these voices inside the
Democratic Party.

New progressive champions on the Democratic Party of Contra Costa County
(CA) Central Committee now include Leslie Baxter, Michelle Simon, Laura
Patch, and Devin T. Murphy.

We’re looking forward to partnering with them to make the party more
transparent and responsive to the grassroots.

Finally, two new progressive committee members are joining the Democratic
Party of Kern County Central Committee - labor organizer Megan Russell and
Ajaib Gill, a lawyer committed to criminal justice reform and education.

These allies will help us push for more progressive policies and push back
against corporate influence and dark money within the party.

★ ★ ★

The 2024 election season is in full swing, and we want to directly hear
from you about candidates you are excited about! Email our political team
at political@ourrevolution.com to nominate candidates for Our Revolution’s
endorsement.

★ ★ ★

★ ★ ★

AIPAC is already starting to spend $100 million to try to unseat pro-Peace
progressives like Rashida Tlaib, Ilhan Omar, Summer Lee, and others.

Get this sticker today to start a conversation about why we must fight
back against this far right lobbying group, which is pushing for even more
money for Netanyahu’s war on Gaza.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/16335?t=1015&akid=5610%2E5885482%2EISWNj7 ]When you buy this sticker for a one-time donation of $10 or a recurring
$5 monthly donation, you’re funding critical work by Our Revolution to
defend strong voices for Peace in Congress. We cannot let big money
silence progressive voices for peace in Congress.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/16335?t=1016&akid=5610%2E5885482%2EISWNj7 ]GET YOUR STICKER HERE

★ ★ ★

As the country’s largest grassroots-funded progressive political action
group, Our Revolution relies on our members to power our work. In 2024, we
must defend our top progressive leaders and elect new ones to join the
fight against the far right and corporate extremists trying to hijack our
democracy.

[ https://act.ourrevolution.com/go/16333?t=1017&akid=5610%2E5885482%2EISWNj7 ]Sign up TODAY with a $10 monthly donation and you’ll get our 2024
sticker pack. For $27 a month, you’ll also get a handy tote bag. And, for
$50 a month, we’ll send you both plus the official Our Revolution T-shirt!

★ ★ ★

name: Heidi Ganahl
subject: The Mountain Minute: 3/23/2024
body:
Your daily rundown of REAL news for REAL Coloradans.
Heidi Ganahl introduces the Rocky Mountain Voice, a new conservative news media platform for Coloradans to be the VOICE of your VALUES.
| |
---
**PRESENTED BY[ROCKYMOUNTAINVOICE.COM](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
| Hello, Here are some top stories as we head into this sunny Spring weekend! This is the Mountain Minute for Saturday, March 23, 2024.
– Heidi Ganahl
---
|
# TODAY'S TOP STORIES
---
[**How Gen Z Can Save America: Isabel Brown to Discuss New Book at CSU, Tuesday March 26**](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) BY RMV STAFF, [ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
| [ ](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
---
Native speaker and rising Gen Z star Isabel Brown is coming to her alma mater Colorado State University to discuss her new book. Hosted by Turning Point USA, this event is set to take place on March 26th at 6pm in Glover 130. Best of all, [tickets are free](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)! Isabel Brown will be promoting her new book, “[The End of the Alphabet: How Gen Z Can Save America](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.).” Through her book, Brown empowers members of her generation to embrace conservative values and rise up to fight for the future. As the largest emerging demographic in American history, Gen Z is poised to shape the cultural direction of our country for generations to come. Brown believes that Gen Z is ready to embrace leadership and make their voices heard. In “The End of the Alphabet,” Brown explores how Gen Z can embrace traditional values in a new-age way and step up to lead. She challenges the notion that young people are too naive or inexperienced to make a difference, arguing that Gen Z has the power, potential, and opportunity to define the future of America. **[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**

[**Congress Unveils $1.2 Trillion Plan to Avert Shutdown**](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
[NEWSMAX](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* Lawmakers introduced a [$1.2 trillion spending package](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) to avoid a government shutdown. Democrats thwarted most policy mandates and budget cuts sought by House Republicans.

* Funding includes defense, Homeland Security, Health, Labor, and much more, with a focus on national defense and aid to Israel. Democrats emphasized increases in humanitarian assistance, Head Start programs, cancer research, and Alzheimer's research.

[**READ MORE**](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
[**Colorado environmental groups file three ballot measures to limit oil and gas industry**](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
[THE COLORADO SUN](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* Leading Colorado environmental groups have filed language for three ballot measures aimed at [limiting the oil and gas industry](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.).

* Governor Jared Polis has failed to curb this escalation, urging both sides to reconsider their aggressive tactics. Rather than engaging in constructive dialogue, the environmental coalition is resorting to heavy-handed ballot initiatives, potentially jeopardizing Colorado's energy independence and economic growth.

* These proposed measures, under the guise of environmental protection, could stifle innovation, undermine job creation, and threaten the state's energy security.

[**READ MORE**](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
[**COMMENTARY: Stacking the deck: Polis appointment cancels out election for District Attorney**](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
[COLORADO PEAK POLITICS](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* Governor Polis filled a sudden vacancy in the judiciary with his appointee, affecting a prosecutor race in the 6th Judicial District. District Attorney Christian Champagne left the position early, allowing Polis to make the appointment.

* The appointment prompted one candidate, Jason Eley, to withdraw from the race, leaving Sean Murray as the only remaining candidate. Critics argue that such appointments allow politicians to bypass voters and stack positions with party insiders.

* Champagne moves to a role in the attorney general's office, while voters lose the opportunity to choose their representative through the electoral process.

[**READ MORE**](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
[**Poll shows who Republicans want as Trump’s VP running mate**](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
[THE CENTER SQUARE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* The Center Square Voters' Voice Poll asked Republican voters about their [preferred vice presidential candidate for Donald Trump](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.).

* Florida Governor Ron DeSantis emerged as the top choice with 18% support. Former Ambassador and South Carolina Governor Nikki Haley followed closely with 17% support.

* Businessman Vivek Ramaswamy and South Carolina Governor Tim Scott tied for third place with 15% support among Republicans. Democratic former Congressman Tulsi Gabbard received 7% support from Republicans, while South Dakota Governor Kristi Noem garnered 6% support.

[**READ MORE**](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
[**Denver has a ‘Newcomer Director’ and a continuing illegal immigration crisis**](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
BY BENNITO L. KELTY, [WESTWORD](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)
* Since December 2022, Denver has faced an influx of migrants from the southern border, totaling nearly 40,000 arrivals, prompting the city to declare an emergency. The response has cost Denver $61 million, with estimates suggesting an additional $120 million may be needed in 2024, placing a significant financial burden on the city.

* Sarah Plastino, the new director of [Denver's Newcomer Program](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.), aims to shift the city's strategy from emergency response to a long-term plan, but there is hesitation amongst conservatives that this would put even more financial burden on the city.

* Denver's reliance on federal assistance underscores the failure of local leadership to address the crisis adequately. The misallocation of funds, with a significant portion directed towards hotel lodging and payroll rather than addressing core issues, highlights the need for more efficient management and accountability within Denver's administration.

**[READ MORE](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)**
**ONLY ON ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE ⤵️** [**Nine petitions filed in 4th District to follow Rep. Buck in office, only Boebert’s verified as sufficient so far**](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) **BY BRIAN PORTER | STATE | MARCH 22** _Nineteen candidates seeking a place on either a Democratic or Republican ballot in a Congressional District primary made the secretary of state’s Tuesday petition deadline, with thus far only four of those petitions verified as sufficient._ [**Libertarians will gather for party’s convention March 23-24 in Colorado Springs**](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.) **BY BRIAN PORTER | STATE | MARCH 22** _The Colorado Libertarian Party’s Convention is this weekend in Colorado Springs at the Masonic Center, 1150 Panorama Dr._ **[Denver’s regional affairs director, former Fox31 reporter to discuss migrants, homeless](https://link.email.rockymountainvoice.)** **BY BRIAN PORTER | DENVER METRO | MARCH 21** _The impact of migrants and the homeless on communities in Colorado will be discussed Thursday, March 28, with Foothill Republicans._ **Feel like you have a unique story or commentary to share?** We'd love to talk with you about becoming an RMV Contributor. Reply to this email (or reach out to [brian](mailto:brian@rockymountainvoice.com)[@rockymountainvoice.com](mailto:brian@rockymountainvoice.com)) or send us a message on social media to chat!
# 🦬 DID YOU KNOW?

On **March 23, 1868** , the University of Colorado was established. AND this is just the fourth time the Colorado men's and women's basketball teams have both been in the NCAA Tournament. It's just the second time both won a game in the tourney. Both won first-round games in 1997. Go Buffs!

clean_body: Your daily rundown of REAL news for REAL Coloradans. Heidi Ganahl introduces the Rocky Mountain Voice, a new conservative news media platform for Coloradans to be the VOICE of your VALUES. | | PRESENTED BY | Hello, Here are some top stories as we head into this sunny Spring weekend! This is the Mountain Minute for Saturday, March 23, 2024. – Heidi Ganahl | TODAY'S TOP STORIES BY RMV STAFF, | Native speaker and rising Gen Z star Isabel Brown is coming to her alma mater Colorado State University to discuss her new book. Hosted by Turning Point USA, this event is set to take place on March 26th at 6pm in Glover 130. Best of all, ! Isabel Brown will be promoting her new book, “.” Through her book, Brown empowers members of her generation to embrace conservative values and rise up to fight for the future. As the largest emerging demographic in American history, Gen Z is poised to shape the cultural direction of our country for generations to come. Brown believes that Gen Z is ready to embrace leadership and make their voices heard. In “The End of the Alphabet,” Brown explores how Gen Z can embrace traditional values in a new-age way and step up to lead. She challenges the notion that young people are too naive or inexperienced to make a difference, arguing that Gen Z has the power, potential, and opportunity to define the future of America. Lawmakers introduced a to avoid a government shutdown. Democrats thwarted most policy mandates and budget cuts sought by House Republicans. Funding includes defense, Homeland Security, Health, Labor, and much more, with a focus on national defense and aid to Israel. Democrats emphasized increases in humanitarian assistance, Head Start programs, cancer research, and Alzheimer's research. Leading Colorado environmental groups have filed language for three ballot measures aimed at . Governor Jared Polis has failed to curb this escalation, urging both sides to reconsider their aggressive tactics. Rather than engaging in constructive dialogue, the environmental coalition is resorting to heavy-handed ballot initiatives, potentially jeopardizing Colorado's energy independence and economic growth. These proposed measures, under the guise of environmental protection, could stifle innovation, undermine job creation, and threaten the state's energy security. Governor Polis filled a sudden vacancy in the judiciary with his appointee, affecting a prosecutor race in the 6th Judicial District. District Attorney Christian Champagne left the position early, allowing Polis to make the appointment. The appointment prompted one candidate, Jason Eley, to withdraw from the race, leaving Sean Murray as the only remaining candidate. Critics argue that such appointments allow politicians to bypass voters and stack positions with party insiders. Champagne moves to a role in the attorney general's office, while voters lose the opportunity to choose their representative through the electoral process. The Center Square Voters' Voice Poll asked Republican voters about their . Florida Governor Ron DeSantis emerged as the top choice with 18% support. Former Ambassador and South Carolina Governor Nikki Haley followed closely with 17% support. Businessman Vivek Ramaswamy and South Carolina Governor Tim Scott tied for third place with 15% support among Republicans. Democratic former Congressman Tulsi Gabbard received 7% support from Republicans, while South Dakota Governor Kristi Noem garnered 6% support. BY BENNITO L. KELTY, Since December 2022, Denver has faced an influx of migrants from the southern border, totaling nearly 40,000 arrivals, prompting the city to declare an emergency. The response has cost Denver $61 million, with estimates suggesting an additional $120 million may be needed in 2024, placing a significant financial burden on the city. Sarah Plastino, the new director of , aims to shift the city's strategy from emergency response to a long-term plan, but there is hesitation amongst conservatives that this would put even more financial burden on the city. Denver's reliance on federal assistance underscores the failure of local leadership to address the crisis adequately. The misallocation of funds, with a significant portion directed towards hotel lodging and payroll rather than addressing core issues, highlights the need for more efficient management and accountability within Denver's administration. ONLY ON ROCKY MOUNTAIN VOICE ⤵️ BY BRIAN PORTER | STATE | MARCH 22 Nineteen candidates seeking a place on either a Democratic or Republican ballot in a Congressional District primary made the secretary of state’s Tuesday petition deadline, with thus far only four of those petitions verified as sufficient. BY BRIAN PORTER | STATE | MARCH 22 The Colorado Libertarian Party’s Convention is this weekend in Colorado Springs at the Masonic Center, 1150 Panorama Dr. BY BRIAN PORTER | DENVER METRO | MARCH 21 The impact of migrants and the homeless on communities in Colorado will be discussed Thursday, March 28, with Foothill Republicans. It's just the second time both won a game in the tourney. Both won first-round games in 1997. body:
Sarah has a strong track record of working toward expanding health care access to every Delawarean – and she is unafraid to stand up to the GOP’s efforts to overturn the progress made. |
---

Team, **Today marks 14 years since the passage of the Affordable Care Act in Congress.** Since its passage, the ACA has made it easier for everyday Americans to get affordable health insurance for more than a decade and counting. Recently, the GOP has promised to revive efforts to repeal the Affordable Care Act, which would rip care away from many of our neighbors. In the Delaware Senate, Sarah has passed legislation expanding access to health care and introduced new legislation that would mark the **largest ongoing investment in Medicaid in Delaware** since the passage of the Affordable Care Act. Sarah has a strong track record of working toward expanding health care access to every Delawarean – and she is unafraid to stand up to the GOP’s efforts to overturn the hard fought progress that’s been made. The passage of the ACA could not have been possible without Members of Congress who believe that health care is a fundamental human right – **_and Sarah will fight to protect and expand on that progress._** That is why our efforts to raise the resources Sarah needs to win this election are so important. **[So on the 14th anniversary of the ACA, will you consider donating $14 to Sarah’s campaign and help us send her to Congress so she can fight for accessible and affordable health care for all?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/81530811/462732357/-1678796822?refcode=em20240323&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NjI0OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1OTliMGFkYS00MWU5LWVlMTEtYWFmMC0wMDIyNDgyMjM3OTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2KyFkBGJwieqBLxfwTxThNsBfs2BoXckFfeBo1T50Eo=)** | [Donate $14](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/81530814/462732360/1887690415?refcode=em20240323&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDcvMS83NjI0OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1OTliMGFkYS00MWU5LWVlMTEtYWFmMC0wMDIyNDgyMjM3OTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=2KyFkBGJwieqBLxfwTxThNsBfs2BoXckFfeBo1T50Eo=)

It’s your support, your ideas, and your participation that help guide our movement to block the authoritarian right, defend and elect working families champions, and build governing power for working people. **With your help over the past several years, we've been able to help elect some of the most progressive members of Congress this country has ever seen.** But all of the progress we’ve made, all of the power we’ve built, is at stake in 2024 — with AIPAC and its cavalry of GOP mega-donors preparing to spend more than $100 million to unseat the WFP champions in Congress we fought so hard to elect. That’s why we’re strategizing to both block and build this year — block the far right and defend our progressives in Congress, while also building the power we need to stand up to special interests and make change in working people’s lives. **With critical primaries starting in just four weeks, we want to know if you’re in it with us** — and what our priorities should be as we move through this election year. clean_body: We wanted to check in on how you are feeling about 2024. | | | Some of the biggest tests of the 2024 election cycle are just around the corner, Peter. Our champions in Congress are facing primaries that kick off as soon as next month — and as we ramp up our organizing in these critical races, it’s important for us to take a few minutes to check in and hear your thoughts on the political moment we’re in right now. Can you take our short March WFP pulse survey to let us know how you’re feeling, and what is motivating you right now? Whether you've completed one of our surveys recently or you're participating for the first time, we would love to hear from you today. You can start by answering the first question here: At the Working Families Party, we value every member of this community. It’s your support, your ideas, and your participation that help guide our movement to block the authoritarian right, defend and elect working families champions, and build governing power for working people. With your help over the past several years, we've been able to help elect some of the most progressive members of Congress this country has ever seen. But all of the progress we’ve made, all of the power we’ve built, is at stake in 2024 — with AIPAC and its cavalry of GOP mega-donors preparing to spend more than $100 million to unseat the WFP champions in Congress we fought so hard to elect. That’s why we’re strategizing to both block and build this year — block the far right and defend our progressives in Congress, while also building the power we need to stand up to special interests and make change in working people’s lives. With critical primaries starting in just four weeks, we want to know if you’re in it with us — and what our priorities should be as we move through this election year. Your answers, along with responses from other WFP members across the country, will help guide our priorities as we block Republicans this year and keep building independent political power for working people. In solidarity, Team WFP Thoughts? Feedback? We want to hear it. Reply directly to this email and let us know. This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you received this email as a forward and want to get the latest updates from the Working Families Party directly, The Working Families Party is the party of the multiracial working class, fighting for a nation that cares for all of us. Wanna learn more? And if you need to update your contact information, Other political groups have corporate donors. But we can pool our resources too. Paid for by Working Families Party National PAC (77 Sands St. #6, Brooklyn, NY, 11201). | | Sent via . _** Which way to the future? **_ It's more than just a motto.
It's how we're going to put Alaska back in the driver's seat of American excellence, with a commonsense conservative, Nancy Dahlstrom, in Congress. name: Alsobrooks HQ
subject: Your Weekly Team Alsobrooks Newsletter:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Alsobrooks for Senate
body:
|
---
[ ](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/2092?t=1&akid=1281%2E52803%2ECY_hdQ)

| | Greetings #TeamAlsobrooks, Last week on the campaign trail was truly incredible. Thank you to everyone who came out to support us from communities across Maryland. Our grassroots movement is flourishing as more and more people join Team Alsobrooks everyday! In case you missed it, here’s what’s been happening for the past week on the campaign trail and beyond: This week, a new Washington Post and UMD poll showed that despite our opponent’s record spending of $38 million, [**the race remains tight**](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/15396?t=7&akid=1281%2E52803%2ECY_hdQ). While our opponent has been trying to buy up the airwaves for the last year, our people-powered movement is showing that Marylanders are too smart for candidates that buy elections. Take a look at our new ad! Angela has spent her career working hard to keep her community safe, and we’re taking that spirit to the Senate! **[Check it out here>>](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/15397?t=8&akid=1281%2E52803%2ECY_hdQ)** [](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/15407?t=30&amount=25&akid=1281%2E52803%2ECY_hdQ) Great to be back in Baltimore with our friend Senator Antonio Hayes to speak with folks about our race. As we’ve traveled across that State, it's been clear that we all want the same things: well-paying jobs, affordable health care, and good schools to send our kids to. By working together we can accomplish all this and more to put our state on the right track. Thanks to Lisa and Chris Summers for organizing a lovely group. [](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/15407?t=31&amount=25&akid=1281%2E52803%2ECY_hdQ) We had a fantastic time at the Baltimore City Central Committee candidates forum. For too long, some communities have been left behind and written off — but we’ll fight for every single Marylander in the Senate. Together, we can build a bright future for all of us right here in Maryland. [](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/15407?t=32&amount=25&akid=1281%2E52803%2ECY_hdQ) Thanks to Apostle Antonio Palmer and First Lady Barbara Palmer for inviting us worship at Kingdom Celebration Center Church in Gambrills, Sunday morning. This morning's sermon had the entire church on their feet, and it truly warmed our hearts to join in praise and worship with so many from the community. Great to see Delegate Shaneka Henson! [](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/15407?t=33&amount=25&akid=1281%2E52803%2ECY_hdQ) We had a fabulous time at the Tri-Chapter Links Black Family Wellness Expo hosted by the Prince George's County, Mount Rose, and Willow Oak Chapters of The Links, Incorporated! They do incredible work in our community by providing crucial resources to families. [](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/15407?t=34&amount=25&akid=1281%2E52803%2ECY_hdQ) We had a blast in Fulton, spreading the word about my campaign for the U.S. Senate. It was so great to be with friend Mike Buch, who brought together a fantastic group of folks to hear about my mission to go farther and do better for the state of Maryland. [](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/15407?t=35&amount=25&akid=1281%2E52803%2ECY_hdQ) We are so grateful to Jessica Strauss and Andre Davis for gathering a fantastic group together in Baltimore for a robust conversation about how we move Maryland forward. It’s always such a pleasure hearing from folks about how we can make our state work for every single resident. Great to see our friend Congressman Sarbanes! [](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/15407?t=36&amount=25&akid=1281%2E52803%2ECY_hdQ) **UPCOMING EVENTS** **Saturday, March 23 (Odenton)** – Knock Doors in Odenton with Rep. Steny Hoyer and County Executive Steuart Pittman –**[RSVP Here](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/13649?t=9&akid=1281%2E52803%2ECY_hdQ)** **Saturday, March 23 (Capitol Heights)** – Men for Alsobrooks Meet and Greet – **[RSVP Here](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/14984?t=10&akid=1281%2E52803%2ECY_hdQ)** **Thursday, April 4th (Annapolis) —** Organizing Meeting! — **[RSVP Here](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/15398?t=11&akid=1281%2E52803%2ECY_hdQ)** **NEWS OF THE WEEK** **[Endorsement Announcement: Columbia Democratic Club](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/15399?t=12&akid=1281%2E52803%2ECY_hdQ)** **[Endorsement Announcement: Delegate Shaneka Henson](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/15400?t=13&akid=1281%2E52803%2ECY_hdQ)** [**WBAL: 'This race is far from over': Political science professor weighs in on status of US Senate Race**
](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/15401?t=14&akid=1281%2E52803%2ECY_hdQ) _"I think in the Democratic race, given how much David Trone has already spent on this race, to only be polling at 34%, and only what I would call a slight lead over Alsobrooks given the number of undecideds, this race is far from over," said Todd Eberly, a political science professor at St. Mary's College of Maryland. "Alsobrooks can close that gap."_ **[American Prospect - The Self-Funding ‘Victim’ of the Criminal Justice System](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/15402?t=15&akid=1281%2E52803%2ECY_hdQ)** _Alsobrooks, on the other hand, has come up through the state ranks and is well known in her county. But she has faced an uphill battle against Trone’s ad blitz. Her campaign’s first ad of the election draws a contrast with Trone by portraying her as a working mother versus the average U.S. senator, who is “64 years old” and “worth $16 million.”_ **[Capital Gazette: Angela Alsobrooks makes play for Baltimore at US Senate Democratic candidate forum](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/15403?t=16&akid=1281%2E52803%2ECY_hdQ)** _“Democrat Angela Alsobrooks made a concerted play for voters at a candidates’ forum in Baltimore... Alsobrooks, the Prince George’s County executive, stressed her ties to the city — she attended the University of Maryland School of Law — and criticized former Republican Gov. Larry Hogan for scrapping the Red Line, a planned Baltimore transit project, in 2015.”_ **[Fox28: Senator Patty Murray makes pro-choice statement with Senate endorsement](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/15404?t=17&akid=1281%2E52803%2ECY_hdQ)** _“[Alsobrooks] will be a strong advocate for every working family across the state of Maryland, stand up for women, and protect reproductive rights. Now more than ever, we need her voice in the Senate.”_ **[Eye on Annapolis: Honorees Announced for 6th Annual Michelle Obama Awards](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/15405?t=18&akid=1281%2E52803%2ECY_hdQ)** _The event will feature a musical selection from Ron-Shaye Clark, a ballet performance by Zorin Perry, award presentations by Office of Equity and Human Rights’ Director Asha Smith, and a keynote address by Prince George’s County Executive Angela Alsobrooks._ **GET INVOLVED** This is a people-powered campaign! What does that mean? It means YOU are the campaign. When you talk with your friends, family, and neighbors about the campaign, that is how we spread the word!

We only have 52 days left to win this race. So if you haven’t gotten involved yet, now is the time! Here are just some ways you can take action:

1. [**Join a volunteer welcome call on Wednesday at 7pm to get started NOW!**](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/10207?t=19&akid=1281%2E52803%2ECY_hdQ)

2. [**Text your friends using our Alsobrooks App!**](http://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/12441?t=20&akid=1281%2E52803%2ECY_hdQ)

3. [**Sign up to watch the polls during Early Vote — May 2nd to May 9th!**](http://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/15324?t=21&akid=1281%2E52803%2ECY_hdQ)

4. [**Sign up to volunteer during GOTV in May!**](http://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/15406?t=22&akid=1281%2E52803%2ECY_hdQ)

5. [**Host an Organizing House Party to spread the word about the campaign!**](http://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/13647?t=23&akid=1281%2E52803%2ECY_hdQ)

| | | [Volunteer](https://alsobrooks.actionkit.com/signup/volunteer-form?t=24&akid=1281%2E52803%2ECY_hdQ)
---
| | [Donate](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/15407?t=37&amount=25&akid=1281%2E52803%2ECY_hdQ)
---
**SOCIALS**
While we'll keep sending emails to keep you updated, the best way to keep up with Angela is on our social media channels. Follow us on Twitter, Facebook, and Instagram to follow along with us on the campaign trail!
| | [Facebook](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/8814?t=25&akid=1281%2E52803%2ECY_hdQ)
---
| | [Twitter](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/8815?t=26&akid=1281%2E52803%2ECY_hdQ)
---
| | [Instagram](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/8816?t=27&akid=1281%2E52803%2ECY_hdQ)
---
Sincerely,
Team Alsobrooks

| | [ ](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/89?t=2&akid=1281%2E52803%2ECY_hdQ)
---

| | We believe that emails are the best way for our organization to stay in touch with supporters like you. But if you'd like to receive fewer emails, [click here.](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/signup/fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=1281%2E52803%2ECY_hdQ)
---
[ ](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/115?t=4&akid=1281%2E52803%2ECY_hdQ)
| | [ Chip in a one-time donation ](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/4693?t=5&akid=1281%2E52803%2ECY_hdQ)
---
| | [ Chip in a monthly recurring donation! ](https://act.angelaalsobrooks.com/go/4694?t=6&akid=1281%2E52803%2ECY_hdQ)
---

| If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please click [here to unsubscribe](https://alsobrooks.actionkit.com/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=28&akid=1281%2E52803%2ECY_hdQ).
| Paid for by Alsobrooks for Senate
---
Alsobrooks for Senate
PO Box 6058
Largo, MD 20792

name: FightForReform.org
subject: Retirement age raised and Social Security delayed?!
disclaimer_text: paid for and provided in kind by End Citizens United Nonfederal, P
body:
Millions of Americans will have their Social Security benefits delayed
| [ ](https://act.fightforreform.org/go/26910?t=1&akid=21054%2E4449941%2EhinhHI)
---
_**We have HORRIBLE news:**_ [ ](https://act.fightforreform.org/survey/raise-retirement-poll?source=FFR_20240323_EMPOLL_E2_T0_GM-14D-AC&t=7&akid=21054%2E4449941%2EhinhHI) If the GOP is successful, _millions of Americans will have their Social Security benefits delayed and will be faced with a terrifying and uncertain future._ | _So we’re launching a MASSIVE poll to see where Democrats stand:_
**[_Do you approve of the GOP’s plan to raise the retirement age?_](https://act.fightforreform.org/survey/raise-retirement-poll?source=FFR_20240323_EMPOLL_E2_T0_GM-14D-AC&t=8&akid=21054%2E4449941%2EhinhHI)** | **[YES, OF COURSE! »](https://act.fightforreform.org/survey/raise-retirement-poll?source=FFR_20240323_EMPOLL_E2_T0_GM-14D-AC&t=9&akid=21054%2E4449941%2EhinhHI)**
---
**[NO, I DON’T »](https://act.fightforreform.org/survey/raise-retirement-poll?source=FFR_20240323_EMPOLL_E2_T0_GM-14D-AC&t=10&akid=21054%2E4449941%2EhinhHI)**
**[I’M NOT SURE »](https://act.fightforreform.org/survey/raise-retirement-poll?source=FFR_20240323_EMPOLL_E2_T0_GM-14D-AC&t=11&akid=21054%2E4449941%2EhinhHI)**
**_WE NEED 250 LOCAL RESPONSES FOR ACCURATE DATA_ – PLEASE RESPOND PROMPTLY BEFORE OUR POLL CLOSES TOMORROW AT 9AM.**
Thank you for sharing your thoughts! We can’t protect Social Security without your response.

Thank you,

What happens here in Oregon will impact the entire country. Here’s why:
House Republicans have just a _four-seat Majority_ , so they’re going all in on swing districts to ensure they can maintain and expand their Majority. And **national Republican dark money groups have already signaled that we are a top target. **
If they succeed in flipping OR-06 red and keeping control of the House, the extreme GOP will have another two years to pass as much extreme, far-right legislation as possible – **including a national abortion ban.**
**So when I say what happens here in Oregon has the power to impact every single person across America, I really mean it.**
That’s why I need your help.
**[If we all work together, I know we can keep Oregon blue and retake the House Majority. That’s why your $1 donation is so critical. So please, can I count on your support?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/81531199/462733785/1032217744?refcode=em_fr_2024.03.23_b1_as_all-nd&amount=1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMzEvMS85Mzg4OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI0NzI0ZGM1ZC00M2U5LWVlMTEtYWFmMC0wMDIyNDgyMjM3OTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=zbLPk3kUMwzUxCwk0_H9KktdLM8oOUxNorQSRMNHIgs=)**

clean_body: Hi – it’s Andrea. Earlier this month, I was proud to announce that I’m officially on the ballot in Oregon’s Sixth Congressional District. Today, I have an important ask of you, friend: = = Hi – it’s Andrea. Earlier this month, I was proud to announce that I’m officially on the ballot in Oregon’s Sixth Congressional District. Today, I have an important ask of you, friend: = | = What happens here in Oregon will impact the entire country. Here’s why: House Republicans have just a four-seat Majority , so they’re going all in on swing districts to ensure they can maintain and expand their Majority. And national Republican dark money groups have already signaled that we are a top target. If they succeed in flipping OR-06 red and keeping control of the House, the extreme GOP will have another two years to pass as much extreme, far-right legislation as possible – including a national abortion ban. So when I say what happens here in Oregon has the power to impact every single person across America, I really mean it. That’s why I need your help. = = Thank you so much, Andrea | | = Andrea Salinas for Oregon PO Box 230985 Tigard, OR 97281 Paid for by Andrea Salinas for Oregon Andrea is fighting for Oregon, fighting for affordable quality health care, an economy that works for everyone, and clean air, land, and water. Now, Andrea's ready to show Washington what hard work can accomplish – real, meaningful change for all of us. | =

body:

In just a few moments, [I’m going to ask you to make your first donation of the year to our grassroots movement, and have it matched _five_ times.](https://an.dlcc.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfquGapMyWi9pdaEixQ440deN98XXDgFQQkqxswzA5hQTbKGAfumYrx1uYV4MfWXkukom0b5VzG0GlQUp468EDm4xwNDx4EvqlV1yVN-b68qxhk4TdGQw0s0-oTIWWnaoYab1NXJKhyxtLrc-V57GV75WbqqUBFFL5fpcO9CpW49Bs6sKMacBpt7E1Vgv120dstlrpdTbXmo48UE40y39EVnFXW5pPsaoBQMVuQlDM8Pi3jlOniXHR9lyT4a1jB5yzG_QkF_z3MTNgVMmwZYrGoBCipYGQglzCq-0_KSxF7HZ9NoTpcbZAKEcofsRaHNM7krq2PfWlsYC97QUSxCgVoO2EQ0z4XzLUqF2jaK9TMUxU6RhiZeDt_FZwCjuT8cvY0pZekLzSLrwgq1D0Yl0lxRNtz6S5tKGLJq4VE48pwdX1qVAW8zWlNW-f3GN5uc83IpkS6SpuOU_cvkXh_SiHvoh61ZipHB_6uZI1vWUTeLQleu2MpHmLOCTkKXruGySdorx1ifnlPBHiV9uwUc-VF_E/44w/7cqMGa0eSeaZTvElJz6ueQ/h0/h001.9QAcIuXth1fdYb6ipq_y0fe7zWD0LyFLHw0Ni8LUZco) But first, I hope you’ll give me a chance to explain why your support _right now_ is so important: We had quite the year in 2023 – state Democrats defied the odds, flipping the Virginia House, expanding our majorities in New Jersey, and _winning over 30_ special elections. **But I need to be honest with you:** Republicans are taking every opportunity to take back control in the states. We expect them to pour _millions of dollars_ into battleground states like Pennsylvania and Michigan, and right now, **we don’t have the funds to compete with their right-wing billionaire backers.** That’s why a coalition of generous donors is matching each donation from now until the 31st _five times_. That means your $7 donation will have a $35 impact to build Democratic power in the states. Emails are a crucial way to stay in touch with our supporters. Please add us to your address book or safe senders list to ensure timely election updates.

clean_body: Republicans are taking every opportunity to take back control in the states. We don’t have the funds to compete with their right-wing billionaire backers. | | | | | ---|--- In just a few moments, But first, I hope you’ll give me a chance to explain why your support right now is so important: We had quite the year in 2023 – state Democrats defied the odds, flipping the Virginia House, expanding our majorities in New Jersey, and winning over 30 special elections. But I need to be honest with you: Republicans are taking every opportunity to take back control in the states. We expect them to pour millions of dollars into battleground states like Pennsylvania and Michigan, and right now, we don’t have the funds to compete with their right-wing billionaire backers. That’s why a coalition of generous donors is matching each donation from now until the 31st five times. That means your $7 donation will have a $35 impact to build Democratic power in the states. | If you've stored your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will process immediately. | | | | | Thank you for fueling this people-powered movement. subject: Head to head with Adam Schiff
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY STEVE GARVEY FOR U
body:
| |
---

| | Dear Fellow American, If you opened this email because you remember my playing days with the Dodgers and the Padres, thank you.
---
| | Having you and all of America's baseball fans cheering for me and Davey Lopes, Bill Russell, Ron Cey and the rest of the guys as we battled Reggie Jackson, Thurman Munson and the Yankees... ...well, as they say, "Those were the days." But this email isn't about baseball. In fact, it's a email I never thought I'd be writing to you.
---
| [](http://links.thegopreport.com/u/click?_t=e3c9a4cac7f248a39c2a3d920479a441&_m=8667d673f903456793822400c5e0e504&_e=AIH0nf2CsQQ464uyIYLWNcM1CkJrYgQU3473tHPWGA49mluq4-qIEIdo8MzPsewRK3wwI6R1zYGKV92Q3CFOiH731E5r3yE-Ljsfs-u9W6DaOaZtJtmUjtUS6gFu1MlhKkqEtxtpUa6nyDxSXsrJVtowmVdBugHpWKRhbWCvQbh2VbCV-ynFBjTU9OlggueC2aadZl1IKqSamD1outtAG9IpAqY_K7YeDlqMytYAt4hVmbLooRhUgjZkoy9cTRLn0kYrUrA6jc5NuQZJiuWf2lPRSAWYcFjahrAOq-7C-xiYas_t8NdWAkv8TtH74mO85-DGBawX7QD8z0bIp47IsEHDVApVAM0-XgmDwtzY52mxaehigdUIL517GdJSsE2vD4FBD3a3pedMOYen6krcRA%3D%3D)
---
| | **[Today, I'm asking you to help me defeat the radical Adam Schiff for a seat in the United States Senate.](http://links.thegopreport.com/u/click?_t=e3c9a4cac7f248a39c2a3d920479a441&_m=8667d673f903456793822400c5e0e504&_e=AIH0nf2CsQQ464uyIYLWNcM1CkJrYgQU3473tHPWGA49mluq4-qIEIdo8MzPsewRK3wwI6R1zYGKV92Q3CFOiH731E5r3yE-Ljsfs-u9W6DaOaZtJtmUjtUS6gFu1MlhKkqEtxtpUa6nyDxSXsrJVtowmVdBugHpWKRhbWCvQbh2VbCV-ynFBjTU9OlggueC2aadZl1IKqSamD1outtAG9IpAqY_K7YeDlqMytYAt4gUvuo17wjFX7-96d64WqaVnNq4MXKpWAf4N1Ah6FgUYfgHFQii59Cp0WMV9PWtVrqxqh6TfvF6d5dxBhTSe44c_ysuzqFI4QGdos8-P-3Kzp3N1aKwelkc7ETg2ffMhyUR-VisauAjZZXOBiktCZcqBCUcRyt2MSbsbcjJZWtRLA%3D%3D)** As of March 5th, it's official. I'm going head-to-head with Adam Schiff in November. Democrat policies have brought America to its knees. There's fear of walking down the street because of rampant crime, homelessness, and drug addiction. The cost of living is exploding and for the first time causing more people to leave California. Joe Biden, Adam Schiff and the rest of the Democrats don't care one bit that Americans are having a hard time. I'd go even further to say they welcome the misery so they can keep folks down and dependent. And that's why I'm running for Senate. **_We Republicans_ pulled our country out of the 1970's Democratic malaise by electing Ronald Reagan.** And I believe we can do it again by putting classic conservative values at the heart of everything we do. But there is no way I can pull it off without you. **[**Will you rush a contribution to my campaign of $35 or more today?**](http://links.thegopreport.com/u/click?_t=e3c9a4cac7f248a39c2a3d920479a441&_m=8667d673f903456793822400c5e0e504&_e=AIH0nf2CsQQ464uyIYLWNcM1CkJrYgQU3473tHPWGA49mluq4-qIEIdo8MzPsewRK3wwI6R1zYGKV92Q3CFOiH731E5r3yE-Ljsfs-u9W6DaOaZtJtmUjtUS6gFu1MlhKkqEtxtpUa6nyDxSXsrJVtowmVdBugHpWKRhbWCvQbh2VbCV-ynFBjTU9OlggueC2aadZl1IKqSamD1outtAG9IpAqY_K7YeDlqMytYAt4gU3z6XAV_LF5AQ_SuiGREdFKOKK4U5w3tyj_KCgecQnKBtUTsEw66_XngD0PPdtbghYYS10sIg9bHWGLorIx98D8QTlelDyu2DLZEdaxWnTOxdtgjz9EWf95qvB-S4OrA-UDw_NnO_mEXnjNNDnsYDAEjKkUeMmjylV9S4S6WZ4Q%3D%3D)** I've lived and worked in California for over 50 years, and I can tell you -\- voters here have had it with the Democrats like I've never seen before. They are hungry for change, and if you and I work together to reach voters in every neighborhood, on every beach, in every corner of California... I know we can turn this historic opportunity into a _**Republican win**_. I'm fighting for the soul of the state I love -- but make no mistake -- I'm not just taking the field for Californians. When we win here... when we rip this seat out the hands of the radical Adam Schiff... **...we start the nationwide Republican Comeback Rally our country desperately needs.** Thank you and God Bless,
---
| | [](http://links.thegopreport.com/u/click?_t=e3c9a4cac7f248a39c2a3d920479a441&_m=8667d673f903456793822400c5e0e504&_e=AIH0nf2CsQQ464uyIYLWNcM1CkJrYgQU3473tHPWGA49mluq4-qIEIdo8MzPsewRK3wwI6R1zYGKV92Q3CFOiH731E5r3yE-Ljsfs-u9W6DaOaZtJtmUjtUS6gFu1MlhKkqEtxtpUa6nyDxSXsrJVtowmVdBugHpWKRhbWCvQbh2VbCV-ynFBjTU9OlggueC2aadZl1IKqSamD1outtAG9IpAqY_K7YeDlqMytYAt4ijia1YyAMNUDmFiaYajTkATk-nzvihFj_gqDfGwpbrPQLHVoN_Fg70348nSvn-oZ4rtVxDOBns52pvYBe_5fT1aKXSoWWHKOU210kBT4F2ENkYP0lJa1E9RGwi9WEqHcxJeJWgVLUjtMCvx4WRkgvaJSPNY2ygCRInPRQLoq5EtQ%3D%3D)

name: Leaders We Deserve HQ
subject: Anna Thomas is a leader we deserve
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY LEADERS WE DESERVE, LEADERSWEDESERVE
body:
She's running to represent Pennsylvania's 137th House District.
# [](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/go/852?refcode=em20240323-1&t=1&akid=488%2E587540%2EO4Zpx3)
Peter,
One of the newly endorsed candidates we’re featuring this week is **Anna Thomas** , who’s running to represent Pennsylvania’s 37th House District.
She’s been serving her community **_since she was in high school_** , serving as a student representative to the Bethlehem Area School Board from 2011-2013!
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-20240323-1?refcode=em20240323-1&amount=5&refcode2=488_587540_O4Zpx3&akid=488%2E587540%2EO4Zpx3)
**[Pitch in $5 to help us elect leaders like Anna this November →](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-20240323-1?refcode=em20240323-1&amount=5&refcode2=488_587540_O4Zpx3&akid=488%2E587540%2EO4Zpx3)**
While studying as a graduate student at the University of Pennsylvania, Anna did research for her thesis in partnership with the Philadelphia Police Department. She also conducted research into the ways US companies can expand energy production while shrinking their impact on the environment.
Anna’s forward-thinking, research-based approach to legislating is exactly the kind of leadership we need if we’re going to make progress on urgent priorities for young people, like **climate change, gun violence, housing costs,** and so much more.
And as a young person herself, Anna understands that we can’t sit back and wait for these issues to fix themselves. She’ll be the leader we need to make change happen — but we need to make sure she wins this November first!
**As a grassroots organization, we don’t take corporate donations. We rely entirely on donors like you to help us support the candidates we’ve endorsed.**
**[So Peter, will you pitch in $5 or anything you can now to help us elect leaders like Anna Thomas up and down the ballot on Election Day?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-20240323-1?refcode=em20240323-1&amount=5&refcode2=488_587540_O4Zpx3&akid=488%2E587540%2EO4Zpx3)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-20240323-1?amount=5&refcode=em20240323-1-488&refcode2=488_587540_O4Zpx3&akid=488%2E587540%2EO4Zpx3)**
Thanks,

Leaders We Deserve HQ
* * *
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/lwd-em-footer?refcode=em20240323-1&t=2&refcode2=488_587540_O4Zpx3&akid=488%2E587540%2EO4Zpx3)**
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is a critical way we communicate with grassroots supporters like you. However, if you would like to change your subscription preferences, here are some options:
* [Receive Fewer Emails](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/receive-fewer-emails/?t=3&akid=488%2E587540%2EO4Zpx3)
* [Update Your Information](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/signup/update-your-information/)
* [Unsubscribe](https://act.leaderswedeserve.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=4&akid=488%2E587540%2EO4Zpx3)

To give by check:
Leaders We Deserve
611 Pennsylvania Ave SE
#192
Washington, DC 20003
Email us: [info@leaderswedeserve.com](mailto:info@leaderswedeserve.com)

The next $510,000/$255,000 from a person/PAC will be split equally among the Democratic state parties from these states: AK, AL, AR, AZ, CA, CO, CT, DC, DE, FL, GA, HI, IA, ID, IL, IN, KS, KY, LA, MA, MD, ME, MI, MN, MO, MS, MT, NC, ND, NE, NH, NJ, NM, NV, NY, OH, OK, OR, PA, RI, SC, SD, TN, TX, UT, VA, VT, WA, WI, WV, and WY. A contributor may designate their contribution for a particular participant by contacting contact@joebiden.com. The allocation formula above may change if following it would result in an excessive contribution. Contributions will be used in connection with federal elections, may be spent on any activity as each committee determines in its sole discretion, and will not be earmarked for any particular candidate. Federal law requires us to use our best efforts to collect and report the name, mailing address, occupation, and name of employer of individuals whose contributions exceed $200 per election cycle. Biden Victory Fund does not accept contributions from corporations or their PACs, unions, federal contractors, national banks, those registered as federal lobbyists or under FARA, SEC-named executives of fossil fuel companies, or foreign nationals.

body:
|
#
Hi everyone, Contrary to popular belief, Tammy Baldwin and I are _not_ the same person (even if reporters, Republican operatives, and sometimes the occasional colleague mix us up!) 👯 [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/81531945/462735985/-83437346?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&refcode=AU_TSM_FR_TAN_GEN_AC90_20240323_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTAvMS83OTU1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJlNGEzNzBmMC00NmU5LWVlMTEtYWFmMC0wMDIyNDgyMjM3OTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=tg9DTrBiCYWktYzV3Uzk_W07j1WBskHFYMgiZtsOiB8=) No matter what, I’m happy to be mistaken for Tammy — and I’m doing all I can to make sure we send her back to the U.S. Senate this year! She has fought tirelessly for affordable healthcare, LGBTQ+ rights, veterans, working families, and so much more. She is deeply committed to making life better for every Wisconsinite, which is why I’m proud to serve alongside her in the Senate. **But Tammy is in for a tough reelection campaign, and her race could decide which party controls the Senate.** _No pressure, Wisconsin!_ Winning this race is not going to be easy. A megamillionaire self-funder is running against Tammy, and he has the support of national Republicans. We’ve got to make sure Tammy has the resources to win this November and protect our Senate majority, so I’m reaching out today to ask: **[Will you split a donation between my friend (and purported lookalike) Tammy Baldwin and my campaign to help reelect her to the Senate? name: Dr. Raul Ruiz
subject: some lessons from my teenage self:
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dr. Raul Ruiz For Congress Committee P
body:
| [ ](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/81532093/462736587/1072501871?refcode=header-img&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDcvMS8xMDc4NzAiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiOGY5MGY1OGUtNDdlOS1lZTExLWFhZjAtMDAyMjQ4MjIzNzk0IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=JNI9S6r9UsgC7dMCPfZFnR7AYndMu_YNar74GrE2aZU=)
---
[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/81532094/462736588/-686654137?amount=10&amounts=10,25,50,100&refcode=RRFC_20240324_EMDF_E1_T0_AC_RS&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDcvMS8xMDc4NzAiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiOGY5MGY1OGUtNDdlOS1lZTExLWFhZjAtMDAyMjQ4MjIzNzk0IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=JNI9S6r9UsgC7dMCPfZFnR7AYndMu_YNar74GrE2aZU=) Folks,

Have I ever told you how I got into public service?

**I grew up the son of hardworking farmworkers.** My family lived in a small trailer in rural California where my brother and I shared a bed that in the daytime converted into a kitchen table.

When I was a teenager, I walked in the desert heat to different businesses asking them to invest in our community by investing in me and my dreams of a college education. I made them a promise that I would come back to the Coachella Valley to serve our community as a physician in return for their trust in me.

I not only returned to serve as an emergency physician, but eventually was elected to serve my community in Congress. For more than a decade, I’ve fought every day for my family, friends, and neighbors who dream the same dream my parents had for me.

**Those memories have come flooding back lately.** Because I’ve spent entirely too much time listening to the MAGA circus go round after round, attacking each other, blaming Democrats, and doing everything they can _EXCEPT_ serving the American people.

**And that’s part and parcel for the extreme MAGA caucus.** Instead of doing the hard work of reaching across the aisle to build consensus, they grind our government to a halt to play partisan games.

Instead of building progress and promise for the next generation of Americans, they desperately scratch and claw their way to their own power, everyone else be damned. **They’re not public servants – they only serve themselves, and I’m sick of it.**

**They need to get their house in order. They need to end the chaos. They need to do their jobs. But I’m not waiting around for them, team, I’m taking action.**

[**I’m taking a lesson from my teenage self here – together we can defeat the MAGA chaos caucus and win back control of the House. But I can’t do it alone. So I’m counting on you here, friend. Can you pitch in $10 (or anything you can afford) toward our 1,000 donation goal today and help us take back our House Majority and end the MAGA chaos? »**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/81532095/462736589/-686654137?amount=10&amounts=10,25,50,100&refcode=RRFC_20240324_EMDF_E1_T0_AC_RS&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDcvMS8xMDc4NzAiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiOGY5MGY1OGUtNDdlOS1lZTExLWFhZjAtMDAyMjQ4MjIzNzk0IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=JNI9S6r9UsgC7dMCPfZFnR7AYndMu_YNar74GrE2aZU=) If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | [CONTRIBUTE $3 »](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/81532101/462736595/-686654137?amount=3&amounts=3,7,10&express_lane=true&refcode=RRFC_20240324_EMDF_E1_T0_AC_RS&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDcvMS8xMDc4NzAiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiOGY5MGY1OGUtNDdlOS1lZTExLWFhZjAtMDAyMjQ4MjIzNzk0IiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=JNI9S6r9UsgC7dMCPfZFnR7AYndMu_YNar74GrE2aZU=)

clean_body: | = = Folks, Have I ever told you how I got into public service? I grew up the son of hardworking farmworkers. My family lived in a small trailer in rural California where my brother and I shared a bed that in the daytime converted into a kitchen table. When I was a teenager, I walked in the desert heat to different businesses asking them to invest in our community by investing in me and my dreams of a college education. I made them a promise that I would come back to the Coachella Valley to serve our community as a physician in return for their trust in me. I not only returned to serve as an emergency physician, but eventually was elected to serve my community in Congress. For more than a decade, I’ve fought every day for my family, friends, and neighbors who dream the same dream my parents had for me. Those memories have come flooding back lately. Because I’ve spent entirely too much time listening to the MAGA circus go round after round, attacking each other, blaming Democrats, and doing everything they can EXCEPT serving the American people. And that’s part and parcel for the extreme MAGA caucus. Instead of doing the hard work of reaching across the aisle to build consensus, they grind our government to a halt to play partisan games. Instead of building progress and promise for the next generation of Americans, they desperately scratch and claw their way to their own power, everyone else be damned. They’re not public servants – they only serve themselves, and I’m sick of it. They need to get their house in order. They need to end the chaos. They need to do their jobs. But I’m not waiting around for them, team, I’m taking action. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | = = = Thank you for being with me in this fight, Dr. Raul Ruiz | | Paid for by Dr. Raul Ruiz For Congress Committee P.O. Peter,
Throughout my career, I’ve been proudly dedicated to leadership and service, holding senior positions at organizations like the Make-A-Wish Foundation and the American Red Cross.
More than anything else, my deep passion for helping others has driven me as I’ve navigated through life. From being on the ground coordinating first-response efforts after natural disasters to supporting families during their most difficult times of crisis, I’ve been able to cross paths with so many people and play a small role in lifting them up when they need it most.
Now, I’m running for Congress because our Democracy is in peril. Can I count on you to help fuel our people-powered campaign? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kkfc-email-q3-2023?refcode=09062023]
CONTRIBUTE [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kkfc-email-q3-2023?refcode=09062023]
Our representative in Congress has held office for over 10 years, yet there have been no improvements made in our district. Instead of working for the great people he’s been elected to serve, David Schweikert has disrespected us by succumbing to special interests and extremist agendas.
I know my experience, along with my dedication to helping others, will allow me to be an effective voice for Arizonans in Congress. Our residents deserve better than career politicians like David Schweikert, so it’s time for us to come together to flip this seat from red to blue.
Peter, will you join our team by making a grassroots contribution today? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/kkfc-email-q3-2023?refcode=09062023]

The first couple of months of 2024 have been busy. I’ve been traveling around Virginia, meeting with community leaders, talking to local business owners, and continuing my service to Virginia’s Seventh District in Congress.

While I’ve been hard at work, I’m happy to know that my campaign team is here, making sure that we have the necessary resources to drive this campaign into our election year.

**Our grassroots support is proof that our message is resonating with voters across the Commonwealth. We need a Governor who brings people together to solve problems and address the urgent needs of Virginians, not leaders who stoke division and hate.**

I know with your support, we’ll flip this Governor’s seat in 2025, and believe it or not, we’re already almost through the first fundraising quarter of 2024. name: Rick Larsen
subject: 45 million Americans could lose their health insurance
disclaimer_text: Paid for and authorized by Citizens to Elect Rick Larsen
body:
The ACA has hit record enrollment — so why does the Chaos Caucus want to undermine their constituents’ care?
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/81532625/462738393/-201694504?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTMvMS84NzY1OSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MGMyZGE4NC00OGU5LWVlMTEtYWFmMC0wMDIyNDgyMjM3OTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=KF6TVS5Z6f4_dd_D4xikyStp3v2KU2eNek126EvurOI=&emci=cd27886b-9fe8-ee11-aaf0-002248223794&emdi=50c2da84-48e9-ee11-aaf0-002248223794&ceid=9494239)
---

| | This weekend marks the anniversary of the passage of the Affordable Care Act (ACA). Fourteen years later, the ACA continues to save lives, expand coverage, and have a positive impact on those who sign up. **Enrollment has hit a historic 21.3 million - opting for ACA marketplace coverage during the Open Enrollment Period.** This statistic includes more than five million people — about a fourth — who are new to the ACA Marketplace and 16 million people who renewed their existing ACA coverage. **With enrollment reaching a record high and millions of American families relying on the ACA for coverage, it simply does not make sense that the Chaos Caucus' agenda is to repeal this landmark legislation.** Rolling back the ACA would not just rip away critical care from the 21.3 million folks using Marketplace plans — **but 45 million Americans could lose their health insurance provided through other provisions of the ACA** — think young adults afforded the option to stay on their parents' plans until age 26, people suffering from pre-existing conditions that could legally be denied care, and more. The far-right's eagerness to roll back health care in America — to a time of runaway corporate profits, coverage denials, and politicians inserting their extreme ideology between you and your health care provider — is not new. Extremists voted upwards of 50 times to repeal the ACA. **Just days ago, some of my Republican colleagues in the House proposed a budget that would destroy this life-saving legislation.** We will not let that stand. I believe health care is a right. As always, we are going to fight back for Washingtonians and the millions of Americans who benefit from the provisions of the ACA. That is why I voted to reduce premiums for ACA members and am actively working to expand access to high-quality, affordable health care that fits Northwest Washington families' budgets. That is why I am continuing to work to lower the costs of prescription drugs. Why I voted to reduce prescription drug costs by letting Medicare negotiate drug prices, by imposing a tax penalty if drug companies increase their prices faster than inflation, and by capping the price of insulin at $35 per month. And that is also why I know that 50-something votes later this is just the beginning. Millions of Americans are counting on us. clean_body: The ACA has hit record enrollment — so why does the Chaos Caucus want to undermine their constituents’ care? | | | | This weekend marks the anniversary of the passage of the Affordable Care Act (ACA). Fourteen years later, the ACA continues to save lives, expand coverage, and have a positive impact on those who sign up. Enrollment has hit a historic 21.3 million - opting for ACA marketplace coverage during the Open Enrollment Period. This statistic includes more than five million people — about a fourth — who are new to the ACA Marketplace and 16 million people who renewed their existing ACA coverage. With enrollment reaching a record high and millions of American families relying on the ACA for coverage, it simply does not make sense that the Chaos Caucus' agenda is to repeal this landmark legislation. Rolling back the ACA would not just rip away critical care from the 21.3 million folks using Marketplace plans — but 45 million Americans could lose their health insurance provided through other provisions of the ACA — think young adults afforded the option to stay on their parents' plans until age 26, people suffering from pre-existing conditions that could legally be denied care, and more. The far-right's eagerness to roll back health care in America — to a time of runaway corporate profits, coverage denials, and politicians inserting their extreme ideology between you and your health care provider — is not new. Extremists voted upwards of 50 times to repeal the ACA. Just days ago, some of my Republican colleagues in the House proposed a budget that would destroy this life-saving legislation. We will not let that stand. I believe health care is a right. As always, we are going to fight back for Washingtonians and the millions of Americans who benefit from the provisions of the ACA. That is why I voted to reduce premiums for ACA members and am actively working to expand access to high-quality, affordable health care that fits Northwest Washington families' budgets. That is why I am continuing to work to lower the costs of prescription drugs. Why I voted to reduce prescription drug costs by letting Medicare negotiate drug prices, by imposing a tax penalty if drug companies increase their prices faster than inflation, and by capping the price of insulin at $35 per month. And that is also why I know that 50-something votes later this is just the beginning. Millions of Americans are counting on us. More soon, Rick | Express Donate: If you've stored your info with ActBlue Express, we'll process your contribution instantly: | = | = | = | = ---|---|---|--- | | Paid for and authorized by Citizens to Elect Rick Larsen. | This email was sent by . Our team is working around the clock to lay out our strategy to mobilize all 15 counties and flip this state Blue in just eight months.
The stakes are clear: everything is on the line this November, and the MAGA agenda is a disaster for working families. Donald Trump has already promised to make cuts to Medicare and Medicaid. And Kari Lake has been bouncing from state to state regurgitating the Big Lie for big checks from wealthy billionaires.
The Biden-Harris Administration is ready to continue the work and finish what they’ve started — that includes investing in and strengthening Medicare, making corporations and billionaires pay their fair share, reducing prescription drug prices, and lowering the costs of raising a family.
We can’t leave any stone unturned — will you add your name and make a commitment to vote this November? [https://secure.ngpvan.com/FK3Fo7JYI0SOAUK_Qo9QIA2]

clean_body: [] The table is set and we need to know: will you commit to vote blue in November, team? COMMIT TO VOTE [] [] Our team is working around the clock to lay out our strategy to mobilize all 15 counties and flip this state Blue in just eight months. The stakes are clear: everything is on the line this November, and the MAGA agenda is a disaster for working families. Donald Trump has already promised to make cuts to Medicare and Medicaid. House Speaker Mike Johnson has already raised MILLIONS in order to expand Republicans’ extremely narrow majority in the House. And Kari Lake has been bouncing from state to state regurgitating the Big Lie for big checks from wealthy billionaires. The Biden-Harris Administration is ready to continue the work and finish what they’ve started — that includes investing in and strengthening Medicare, making corporations and billionaires pay their fair share, reducing prescription drug prices, and lowering the costs of raising a family. We can’t leave any stone unturned — will you add your name and make a commitment to vote this November? [] COMMIT TO VOTE [] Thanks for all you do, — Arizona Democratic Party Arizona Democratic Party PO Box 36123 Phoenix, AZ 85067-6123 United States info@azdem.org [info@azdem.org] Paid for by Arizona Democrats
